description: A university rocketry team has severely damaged the 4-inch lower fin can of their high-power rocket during a hard landing, though the upper payload section remains intact. Reconstruct the rocket design from /home/ga/Documents/rockets/dual_parachute_deployment.ork by replacing the destroyed 101.6mm Aft Body Tube with a stepped-down booster assembly. The new configuration must feature a transition from 101.6mm to 57.4mm (length ~150mm) and a 57.4mm OD booster (length ~600mm) equipped with a 54mm motor. Ensure the new fin design provides a stability margin of at least 1.0 calibers. Save the modified design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/reconstructed_rocket.ork after running a flight simulation. Document the original and new booster diameters, the motor selected, and the achieved stability margin in an engineering memo at /home/ga/Documents/exports/reconstruction_memo.txt.
